topic: Theme 2: The UK Economy - Performance and Policies
subject: economics

items.correct: Unemployment caused by a mismatch between workers' skills/location and available jobs — often due to long-term economic change like deindustrialisation.
items.misconception: Structural unemployment is unemployment caused by a recession.
items.random_wrong: Structural unemployment is when people are too lazy to work.
items.why: Recession-driven unemployment is CYCLICAL. Structural is about long-term mismatches — coal miners can't easily become app developers. Students collapse all unemployment into 'lack of demand'.

items.q: What are the functions of money?
items.correct: Medium of exchange, store of value, unit of account, and standard of deferred payment — money must perform these to function as money.
items.misconception: Money is valuable because it is made of valuable materials.
items.random_wrong: Money is only valuable if backed by gold.
items.why: Modern fiat money has no intrinsic value — its value comes from social acceptance and government issuance. Students think paper money 'must' be backed by something; it isn't, and hasn't been since 1971.

items.correct: A cost imposed on a third party not involved in the transaction — e.g. pollution from a factory affects neighbours who didn't choose to bear it.
items.misconception: A negative externality is something bad that happens to a business.
items.random_wrong: A negative externality is a tax on a business.
items.why: The 'externality' is external to the market participants — borne by THIRD parties. Students miss the structure: producer, consumer, then someone else who didn't agree to be affected.

items.q: The Laffer curve was popularised in:
items.why: It's 1974. “1954” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Solow's growth model shows long-run growth driven by:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Technological progress. “Capital accumulation” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did Solow publish his growth model?
items.why: The real figure is 1956 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
